$statusLegend.ported: byte-equivalent to upstream (CI fails on drift)
$statusLegend.adapted: TypeScript port and/or namespace swap; logically equivalent
$statusLegend.extended: fork adds behavior on top of upstream
$statusLegend.added: file unique to the fork (no upstream)

files.notes: name + textdomain swapped to the artisanpack namespace; attribute schema + supports carried verbatim.

Upstream renders through @wordpress/server-side-render (POSTs to wp/v2/block-renderer/core/archives); this package's preview endpoint is /visual-editor/api/blocks/preview and archives are resolved server-side, so the fork previews through the package's <ServerSideRender> seam and ports the inspector controls (display as dropdown, show counts, group by) to PanelBody. Mirrors the V1 taxonomy-archive override's ArchivesEdit.
Dynamic block — returns null; markup produced by ArchivesBlock::render().
Upstream has no transforms.js; the fork adds bidirectional core/archives ↔ artisanpack/archives block transforms for the rollout window.
Adapted to the visual-editor auto-discovery contract; upstream PHP-side init wiring not carried.

Server renderer reimplemented against cms-framework's Post model (a DynamicBlock) rather than WP_Query. name() renamed core/archives → artisanpack/archives at the I6 fork (mirroring LatestPostsBlock at I4).

knownDivergences: edit.tsx previews via the package's <ServerSideRender> seam instead of upstream's @wordpress/server-side-render (different preview endpoint).
Inspector ported to PanelBody instead of upstream's ToolsPanel; the Group by control offers monthly/yearly only (the server renderer buckets by month or year), not upstream's weekly/daily.

archives is a dynamic block, so (like core/categories, core/tag-cloud, artisanpack/latest-posts, artisanpack/form) it is handled by the Blade DynamicBlockRegistry and is not listed in packages/renderer-parity.json — the React/Vue static renderers do not register it.
transforms.ts adds bidirectional core/archives ↔ artisanpack/archives block transforms that don't exist upstream — required to coexist with core/archives during the V1 rollout.
